title: Can injecting lips trigger a cold sore, and what can we do about it？ | Dr Tim's Tips
filename: Can injecting lips trigger a cold sore, and what can we do about it？ ｜ Dr Tim_s Tips.en.vtt
excerpt: Can lip filler trigger a cold sore i don't think it's the filler itself it's most likely the trauma caused by the needle [Music] so the procedure itself can trigger a cold sore and although this is a very hard thing to get hard data on it does make sense that you may be able to l…

segments.text: can lip filler trigger a cold sore i don't think it's the filler itself it's most likely the trauma caused by the needle [Music] so the procedure itself can trigger a cold sore and although this is a very hard thing to get hard data on it does make sense that you may be able to limit the risk of this through causing less trauma while you inject so it could be just as simple as doing fewer injections i think you're much more likely to get cold sores if you've gone in and out of the lip you know 50 times like you might see with some techniques than if you've gone in twice like you've seen with other techniques similarly you might experiment the idea that you could use a cannula not actually touch the lip at all so you enter lateral to the oral commissure and treat the lip that way that might reduce your chance of getting cold sores and of course you can use prophylaxis so you can prescribe acyclophere before the procedure usually 24 to 40 hours before you start the course of acyclovir then you do the treatment and continue for two or three days afterwards and that may prevent you getting a cold sore so it depends on the patient depends how frequently they

do them and it depends on your ability to prevent or use different techniques these are just ideas that you can use based on the principle that it's the trauma to the vermilion border in particular that causes it um but very hard to be sure i just don't think it makes any sense that the presence of filler itself causes the problem it's the inflammation and the trauma so reduce that and you've got a chance of reducing the chance of cold sores as well as the medical options that you've got hope that helps

transcript: can lip filler trigger a cold sore i don't think it's the filler itself it's most likely the trauma caused by the needle [Music] so the procedure itself can trigger a cold sore and although this is a very hard thing to get hard data on it does make sense that you may be able to limit the risk of this through causing less trauma while you inject so it could be just as simple as doing fewer injections i think you're much more likely to get cold sores if you've gone in and out of the lip you know 50 times like you might see with some techniques than if you've gone in twice like you've seen with other techniques similarly you might experiment the idea that you could use a cannula not actually touch the lip at all so you enter lateral to the oral commissure and treat the lip that way that might reduce your chance of getting cold sores and of course you can use prophylaxis so you can prescribe acyclophere before the procedure usually 24 to 40 hours before you start the course of acyclovir then you do the treatment and continue for two or three days afterwards and that may prevent you getting a cold sore so it depends on the patient depends how frequently they do them and it depends on your ability to prevent or use different techniques these are just ideas that you can use based on the principle that it's the trauma to the vermilion border in particular that causes it um but very hard to be sure i just don't think it makes any sense that the presence of filler itself causes the problem it's the inflammation and the trauma so reduce that and you've got a chance of reducing the chance of cold sores as well as the medical options that you've got hope that helps
